title: allium sphaerocephalon subsp. arvense
intro: Allium sphaerocephalon subsp. arvense, also known as the field ball onion or ball-headed garlic, is a plant species belonging to the Allium genus and is commonly found in Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its high sulfur content. The health benefits of Allium sphaerocephalon subsp. arvense include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
